title: 9 Herbal Creams For Hemorrhoids
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to soothe and relieve hemorrhoids. These creams work by applying a mixture of natural ingredients directly to the affected area, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain. Herbal teas can also help, but in a different way. Drinking herbal teas can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing from the inside out.  Some herbs are particularly effective in relieving hemorrhoids. For example, Hamamelis virginiana, also known as witch hazel, is a natural astringent that helps to reduce swelling and itching. Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, is another popular choice, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin. Calendula officinalis, or marigold, is also effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.  Using herbal creams or drinking herbal teas can bring several benefits to your life. For one, they can help to reduce the frequency and severity of hemorrhoid flare-ups. This can be especially helpful if you experience recurring or persistent hemorrhoids. Additionally, herbal creams and teas can be a natural and non-invasive way to manage symptoms, without the need for harsh chemicals or surgeries. By incorporating herbal remedies into your daily routine, you can take a proactive approach to managing your hemorrhoids and improving your overall health and wellbeing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams contains boiactive constituents such as tannins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort. The tannins in Hamamelis virginiana creams also help to constrict blood vessels, which can help to reduce bleeding and promote healing. Additionally, the astringent properties of flavonoids help to dry up excess mucous and reduce irritation, making it easier to manage hemorrhoid symptoms. By using Hamamelis virginiana creams, individuals may find that their symptoms of hemorrhoids are alleviated, allowing for a faster recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Hamamelis virginiana herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Hamamelis virginiana flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and a double boiler.
Combine the Hamamelis virginiana flowers and coconut oil in the double boiler. Heat the mixture on low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil turns green.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate bowl, melt the beeswax in the microwave or in a saucepan over low heat. Add the melted beeswax to the Hamamelis virginiana mixture.
Add the vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with hemorrhoids. The aloe-emodin in aloe barbadensis creams has been shown to have astringent properties, which can help reduce bleeding and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory effects of aloin and acemannan also help to calm irritated tissues and reduce discomfort. By applying aloe barbadensis creams to the affected area, the bioactive constituents can work together to provide relief from the symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the aloe vera gel and shea butter to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir until well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and add the vitamin E oil. Let it cool and set completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin around hemorrhoids. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have potent antioxidant effects that reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area. The carotenoids, such as beta-carotene, promote wound healing and tissue repair, which is essential for resolving hemorrhoid symptoms. By reducing inflammation, preventing infection, and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate hemorrhoid discomfort and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel.
Dry the Calendula flowers in a low oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours or until crispy.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Calendula flowers, vitamin E oil, and aloe vera gel to the melted oil mixture. Stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool. Pour the mixture into a container and let it set at room temperature. Once set, it's ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams contains allantoin, aloe-emodin, and rosmarinic acid. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Allantoin helps to reduce swelling and promote tissue repair, while aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory effects that can ease pain and discomfort. Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The combination of these properties in Symphytum officinale creams can help to provide relief from the symptoms of hemorrhoids, such as itching, pain, and swelling.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Symphytum officinale roots, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Dry the Symphytum officinale roots in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler, then add 2 tablespoons of the ground Symphytum officinale powder.
Remove from heat, let cool, and pour into a container. Let it set and harden before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically helenalin and 11β-hydroxyhelenalin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the affected area. The flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the tissue from further damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Arnica montana creams may help alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Arnica montana oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted oil and beeswax from heat and stir in the Arnica montana oil and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or jars and let it cool and harden completely.
Apply a small amount of the Arnica montana cream to the affected area as needed to help soothe and relieve hemorrhoid symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus creams contains tannins and anthocyanins, which are powerful antioxidants. These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them beneficial for people with hemorrhoids. The astringent properties of tannins also help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing relief from discomfort and pain. In addition, Vaccinium myrtillus creams contain ellagic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin. By applying Vaccinium myrtillus creams to the affected area, the combination of these active constituents can help to alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium myrtillus herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium myrtillus berries, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Mash the Vaccinium myrtillus berries in a bowl to get the juice.
In a double boiler, melt the coconut oil and beeswax. Remove from heat when melted.
Add the vitamin E oil and berry juice to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, use as hemorrhoids cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum creams contains a compound called aescin, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Aescin has a powerful effect on reducing swelling and inflammation of hemorrhoidal tissues, making it easier to manage symptoms. The cream also contains other bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids and saponins, which contribute to its ability to calm and protect the affected area. These compounds help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, while also promoting healing and tissue repair. Regular application of Aesculus hippocastanum creams can help to reduce the severity and frequency of hemorrhoidal episodes.
remedies.image_alt: Aesculus hippocastanum herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Aesculus hippocastanum leaves, 2 cups of olive oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Heat the olive oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Aesculus hippocastanum leaves to the olive oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a small saucepan and heat until the wax is melted. Pour into a jar and let it cool to form the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hyperforin and n-feruloyltyramine as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce swelling and prevent infection in hemorrhoids. The flavonoids, including rutin and quercetin, in Hypericum perforatum creams improve blood vessel function and promote the healing of damaged tissues. The plant's extracts also have a soothing effect on the affected area, reducing itching and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids. The antiseptic properties of Hypericum perforatum creams help prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, pressing on the solids to get as much liquid as possible.
Mix vitamin E oil into the liquid and pour the mixture into small containers. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides, caftaric acid, and chicoric acid as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with hemorrhoids. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams can also help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the affected area. Chicoric acid has been shown to have a soothing effect on irritated tissues, which can help to calm the discomfort and itching caused by hemorrhoids. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea creams may provide relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for hemorrhoids
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted oil and beeswax mixture. Let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent hemorrhoids from forming by soothing the digestive system and reducing inflammation. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the veins in the rectal area. This can help reduce the pressure and discomfort that can lead to hemorrhoids.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for hemorrhoids can be a helpful relief, but consuming them daily may not be safe for everyone. Some herbs, like witch hazel or aloe vera, can cause stomach upset or interact with medications if taken excessively. It's best to use them in moderation, as needed, to avoid potential side effects.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to show results in reducing hemorrhoid symptoms within a few days to a week. Some people notice improvement in swelling and discomfort after a few days, while others may take a little longer. It's essential to drink the teas regularly to see consistent results.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hemorrhoids in the morning can help reduce symptoms throughout the day. This is because the active ingredients in the tea have time to take effect, reducing inflammation and soothing the affected area. A gentle morning tea can provide relief and help manage discomfort.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas may interact with prescription medications for hemorrhoids. For example, taking certain herbal teas with blood thinners like warfarin can increase the risk of bleeding. Some herbal teas, like ginkgo biloba and echinacea, may also affect blood pressure or interact with pain medications. This can worsen hemorrhoid symptoms or cause other problems.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have hemorrhoids. Some herbs can stimulate the uterus, which isn't safe during pregnancy. If you still want to try them, look for teas that are specifically labeled as safe for pregnant women. Even then, start with small amounts to see how your body reacts.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's generally safe to drink herbal teas for hemorrhoids. However, some herbal teas may affect milk production or pass into breast milk. Look for teas that have been studied for safety while breastfeeding, like peppermint or chamomile. Choose teas that are free of additives and caffeine.
